questions.question: Across the NDT methods, which statement about visual testing is TRUE?
options: It is the newest method and is used least often
It can size flaws deep inside solid material without other methods
It is the oldest method and is usually performed first and on 100% of the work
It can only be performed after radiographic testing
questions.explanation: VT predates every instrument and, on most jobs, is the first examination performed and the only one applied to the entire piece, while other methods sample selected areas.

questions.question: Which item represents the MOST RECENT stage in the evolution of visual testing tools?
options: A hand mirror used to see around a corner
A rigid borescope with lenses and prisms
A flexible fiberscope using coherent fibers
A drone-mounted digital camera with saved digital records
questions.explanation: Tools evolved from simple mirrors and magnifiers, to rigid borescopes, to flexible fiber optics and video probes, and most recently to robotic crawlers, drones, and fully digital records.

questions.question: An indication produced by an intended geometric feature of the part - such as a designed change in section - rather than by a flaw, is best described as:
options: A false indication
A nonrelevant indication
A defect
A rejectable discontinuity
questions.explanation: A nonrelevant indication is caused by part geometry or condition rather than a discontinuity; a false indication is an artifact, and a defect is a discontinuity evaluated as rejectable.

questions.question: The fovea provides the sharpest, most color-accurate vision because it is densely packed with:
options: Cones
Rods
Optic-nerve fibers
Iris muscle
questions.explanation: The fovea is a small central pit packed with cones, giving high-detail, full-color (photopic) vision; the inspector must aim it at the target and scan deliberately.

questions.question: An inspector steps from bright sunlight into a dimly lit tank to perform direct VT. To examine reliably, the FIRST thing he should account for is:
options: Inspecting immediately, since dim surfaces cannot be improved
Staring at his flashlight to reset his eyes
Allowing about 30 minutes of dark adaptation while setting up supplemental light
Lowering the light to reduce eye strain
questions.explanation: Rods need roughly 30 minutes to reach full sensitivity, so a dim examination cannot be rushed; bring controlled supplemental light and allow the eyes to adapt.

questions.question: A procedure requires 100 footcandles minimum for critical direct VT. Approximately what is this in lux, and how is it confirmed?
options: About 10 lux, estimated by eye
About 1080 lux (roughly 1000 lux), measured at the surface with a calibrated meter
About 100 lux, measured at the lamp
About 10,000 lux, assumed from the work lights
questions.explanation: Since 1 footcandle is about 10.76 lux, 100 fc is roughly 1080 lux (commonly cited as about 1000 lux), and it must be verified with a calibrated meter at the examination surface.

questions.question: A bright reflection bouncing off a polished surface straight back into the inspector's eye, hiding what lies beneath it, is called:
options: Glare
Dark adaptation
Accommodation
Diffuse reflection
questions.explanation: Glare is a mirror-like reflection that can completely mask underlying features; a more diffuse light or a change of angle reduces it so the surface can be read.

questions.question: Under common codes such as ASME Section V, Article 9, mirrors, magnifiers, and telescopes used to assist an examination are classified as:
options: Remote (indirect) visual testing equipment
Aids to direct visual examination
Volumetric testing tools
Replacements for adequate lighting
questions.explanation: These tools keep the optical path to the eye unbroken - they only bend or enlarge the view - so they are treated as aids to direct examination, not as remote methods.

questions.question: When a remote (indirect) visual method is substituted for direct examination, the procedure generally requires the remote system to:
options: Use the brightest possible light only
Be operated only by a Level III
Demonstrate resolution at least equal to what direct viewing would provide
Cover twice the area in half the time
questions.explanation: A remote method standing in for direct VT must be shown - not assumed - to deliver resolution at least equal to direct viewing, for example against a resolution target at the working distance.

questions.question: Corrosion products that appear as reddish-brown, green, or whitish deposits are interpreted primarily by:
options: Their temperature
Their color, which is why color perception is screened during qualification
Their magnetic response
Peripheral (rod) vision in the dark
questions.explanation: Corrosion type is often read from color, just like heat tint and penetrant indications, so adequate color perception and color-faithful lighting are required for reliable interpretation.
